problem_text_cn: 4-8 设想全世界所有的人都在赤道上自西向东，以接近百米世界纪录的速度跑步，试估算地球日长将增长还是缩短多少秒？已知质量$m$、半径$R$的匀质球绕其直径以角速度$\omega$旋转时，相对球心的角动量为$\frac{2}{5}mR^{2}\omega$。
problem_text_en: 4-8 Suppose all people in the world are running from west to east on the equator at a speed close to the 100‑meter world record. Estimate whether the length of a day on Earth will increase or decrease, and by how many seconds? Given that for a uniform sphere of mass $m$ and radius $R$ rotating about its diameter with angular velocity $\omega$, the angular momentum relative to the center of the sphere is $\frac{2}{5}mR^{2}\omega$.
problem_solution_cn:
解 世界人口数约算为50亿，人平均质量估算为50kg，总质量为
$$
m=2.5\times 10^{11}kg.
$$
地球初始角速度
$$
\omega_{0}=\frac{2\pi}{T_{0}}.
$$
人跑步速度估算为$v=10m/s$，相对地球圆运动角速度为
$$
\omega^{\prime}=\frac{v^{\prime}}{R_{E}}=\frac{10}{6.4\times 10^{6}}s^{-1},
$$
地球新的自转角速度和周期分记为$\omega_{E}$和$T_{E}$，则有
$$
\omega_{E}=\frac{2\pi}{T_{E}},
$$
人相对地心参考系的角速度为$\omega_{E}+\omega^{\prime}$，由角动量守恒，相继得
$$
\frac{2}{5}m_{E}R_{E}^{2}\omega_{E}+mR_{E}^{2}\left(\omega_{E}+\omega^{\prime}\right)=\frac{2}{5}m_{E}R_{E}^{2}\omega_{0},
$$
$$
\frac{2}{5}m_{E}R_{E}^{2}\omega_{0}-\left(\frac{2}{5}m+m\right)R_{E}^{2}\omega_{E}=mR_{E}^{2}\omega^{\prime},
$$
近似有
$$
mR_{E}^{2}\omega^{\prime}=\frac{2}{5}m_{E}R_{E}^{2}\left(\omega_{0}-\omega_{E}\right)=\frac{2}{5}m_{E}R_{E}^{2}\frac{2\pi\left(T_{E}-T_{0}\right)}{T_{0}T_{E}},
$$
解得
$$
T_{E}-T_{0}=\frac{5}{4\pi}\frac{m}{m_{E}}\omega^{\prime}T_{0}T_{E}\approx\frac{5}{4\pi}\frac{m}{m_{E}}\omega^{\prime}T_{0}^{2}.
$$
将$m$，$\omega^{\prime}$和$m_{E}=6.0\times 10^{24}kg$，$T_{0}=24\times 3600s$等数据代入后，可算得
$$
T_{0}-T_{E}\approx 2.0\times 10^{-10}s,
$$
即地球日长（自转周期）增长$2.0\times 10^{-10}s$（0.2ns），这将是微不足道的变化。
problem_solution_en:
Solution Approximate the world population as 5 billion, average mass per person as 50 kg, giving a total mass
$$
m=2.5\times 10^{11}kg.
$$
The initial angular velocity of the Earth is
$$
\omega_{0}=\frac{2\pi}{T_{0}}.
$$
Take the running speed as $v=10m/s$; the angular velocity relative to the Earth’s circular motion is
$$
\omega^{\prime}=\frac{v^{\prime}}{R_{E}}=\frac{10}{6.4\times 10^{6}}s^{-1}.
$$
Denote the new rotational angular velocity and period of the Earth as $\omega_{E}$ and $T_{E}$, respectively; then
$$
\omega_{E}=\frac{2\pi}{T_{E}}.
$$
The angular velocity of a person relative to the geocentric reference frame is $\omega_{E}+\omega^{\prime}$. From angular‑momentum conservation, we obtain successively
$$
\frac{2}{5}m_{E}R_{E}^{2}\omega_{E}+mR_{E}^{2}\left(\omega_{E}+\omega^{\prime}\right)=\frac{2}{5}m_{E}R_{E}^{2}\omega_{0},
$$
$$
\frac{2}{5}m_{E}R_{E}^{2}\omega_{0}-\left(\frac{2}{5}m+m\right)R_{E}^{2}\omega_{E}=mR_{E}^{2}\omega^{\prime},
$$
approximately
$$
mR_{E}^{2}\omega^{\prime}=\frac{2}{5}m_{E}R_{E}^{2}\left(\omega_{0}-\omega_{E}\right)=\frac{2}{5}m_{E}R_{E}^{2}\frac{2\pi\left(T_{E}-T_{0}\right)}{T_{0}T_{E}},
$$
solving gives
$$
T_{E}-T_{0}=\frac{5}{4\pi}\frac{m}{m_{E}}\omega^{\prime}T_{0}T_{E}\approx\frac{5}{4\pi}\frac{m}{m_{E}}\omega^{\prime}T_{0}^{2}.
$$
Substituting the data $m$, $\omega^{\prime}$, $m_{E}=6.0\times 10^{24}kg$, $T_{0}=24\times 3600s$, we obtain
$$
T_{0}-T_{E}\approx 2.0\times 10^{-10}s,
$$
i.e., the length of a day (rotation period) increases by $2.0\times 10^{-10}s$ (0.2 ns), which is a negligible change.
img_cn: 图片展示了题号为4-8的物理估算题，题目要求估算若全世界所有人以接近百米世界纪录的速度在赤道上跑步，地球日长的变化。图片中包含了完整的解题过程，包括对世界总人口、人平均质量、总质量的估算，地球初始角速度的计算，人跑步的角速度估算，以及利用角动量守恒和匀质球转动惯量公式推导地球自转周期变化量的详细步骤。最后得出地球日长增长约$2.0\times 10^{-10}s$的结论。
img_en: The image shows a physics estimation problem numbered 4-8, asking to estimate the change in the length of a day on Earth if all people in the world run on the equator at a speed close to the 100‑meter world record. The image contains the complete solution process, including the estimation of the world population, average mass per person, total mass, calculation of Earth’s initial angular velocity, estimation of the angular velocity of a person running, and detailed steps using conservation of angular momentum and the formula for the moment of inertia of a uniform sphere to derive the change in Earth’s rotation period. The conclusion states that the length of a day increases by about $2.0\times 10^{-10}s$.
